No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course topic 'Bienestar y ocio' (Well-being and leisure) provides a direct, albeit minor, link to promoting mental health and well-being (Target 3.4).

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly contributes to tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops communicative competence and practical language skills for employment (Target 4.4).
evidence: Abilities of professional research in language

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: While language skills can support employment, the course does not explicitly address decent work conditions or economic growth.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course topic 'El mundo de las nuevas tecnologías' (The world of new technologies) provides a minimal, inferred link to discussing innovation and ICT (Target 9.5, 9.c).
evidence: El mundo de las nuevas tecnologías

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to reducing inequalities based on various statuses.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course topic '¿Pueblo o ciudad?' involves discussing urban and rural living, which could tangentially touch upon aspects of sustainable urbanization or community planning (Target 11.3).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to responsible consumption or production patterns.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to climate change or action.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to marine life or ocean conservation.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course teaches skills like describing problems, offering advice, and expressing disagreement, which are foundational for participatory decision-making and peaceful dialogue (Target 16.7).
evidence: express disagreement and set conditions when handling situations

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course develops 'Abilities of global mobility' (具備國際交流基本知能), a key skill for fostering international cooperation, knowledge sharing, and multi-stakeholder partnerships (Targets 17.6, 17.16).
evidence: 具備國際交流基本知能
